passage: Temperatures dropped below freezing for five consecutive nights in late March. _______ many of the early-blooming flowers in the garden did not survive.
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?
explanation: Freezing temperatures are a direct cause of flowers dying. The passage establishes a clear cause (below-freezing temperatures) and effect (flowers did not survive), making 'As a result' the only logical transition.

distractorRationale.A: 'For instance' introduces an example to illustrate a broader point, but the flower deaths are a consequence of the freezing, not an example of it.
distractorRationale.B: 'Alternatively' presents a different option or possibility, which has no logical connection to the cause-effect structure of the passage.
distractorRationale.C: 'Likewise' signals similarity between two parallel situations, but the passage describes a single cause leading to an effect, not two comparable events.
distractorRationale.D: Correct. 'As a result' accurately captures the causal relationship between freezing temperatures and flower death.
